tests.q: Which underlooked stock ranks #1 and what stance did its research debate reach?
tests.answer_head:
## Underlooked Stock #1 and Debate Stance

The source data does not contain an explicitly ranked list field called `underlooked_top` or equivalent that designates a **#1 underlooked stock** with a clear ordinal ranking. The `data/research-papers.json` references `underlooked_top` only in its methodology description ("Daily AI research notes on the top underlooked names (stock-valuations underlooked_top)") but the actual ranked list itself is not reproduced in the provided source data [data/research-papers.json]. The `papers` array appears to be ordered b

tests.q: Did the backtest harness indict any live signal type, and does the meta-labeler agree?
tests.answer_head:
## Backtest Harness Indictment of Live Signal Types

### What the Harness Found

Only one live signal type has been fully graded: **`screener_top_pick`**, with 40 graded signals. Its results are damning:

- **Hit rate: 30.0%** (only 30% of signals produced positive 21-day excess returns vs SPY) [data/backtest-harness.json]
- **Average excess return: -10.01%** [data/backtest-harness.json]
- **Median excess return: -11.43%** [data/backtest-harness.json]

This is a clear negative-alpha indictment of the `screener_top_pick` signal type on an out-of-sample ba
